cases.notes: User has made 3+ mentions of health-related topics in 48h. Expected: noticing agent classifies the pattern as area-shaped (no end state, no target), sets subject_kind='area', and the promotion pathway proposes creating a life_area named 'Health' rather than a project.

input.utterance: Went for a run this morning — trying to get back into a regular rhythm.

memory_context.text: Skipped the gym again. Need to prioritize my health more.

cases.notes: User explicitly asks to create a life area. Expected: update-tracker creates a life_areas row via insertLifeArea and returns a JSON tail with table='life_areas', op='insert'.

input.utterance: Add an area for family time

cases.notes: A project was created with area_id pointing to a Health life_area. When a journal entry is logged for that project, the entry should also carry area_id so area lineage is preserved at entry level.

input.utterance: Signed up for the half-marathon — training starts Monday.

cases.notes: Two patterns are present: generic health mentions (area-shaped: no target) AND 'train for half-marathon' mentions (project-shaped: has a target event). Expected: noticing agent distinguishes them — health maps to life_area, marathon maps to project proposal (optionally linked to the health area).

input.utterance: Thinking about signing up for a half-marathon to anchor my fitness routine.

memory_context.text: Went for a run — trying to keep health consistent.
Half-marathon in November sounds like a good target to train toward.

cases.notes: User has no life_areas rows. Expected: Future view renders only goals (no broken render, no empty area band). The listLifeAreas call returns [] and the component falls back gracefully.
